course_description: This course builds on the translation skills developed in the first year of the program and focuses on the idea of adaptation and 'transcreation'. Topics to be covered include audio-visual translation and the translation of advertisement materials, and students will explore creative solutions for various settings that involve specific constraints and requirements.

Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice. Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable. Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full name,

the Course Code

course_aims: Students will be trained to develop creative translation skills and abilities to identify and analyse translation issues specific to the field, enabling them to devise creative solutions through versatile and critical thinking.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop a solid understanding of constrained translation, adaptation and transcreation
Identify and analyse translation issues involved in the translation of specialised texts with highly creative content
Identify and apply appropriate theoretical approaches in order to devise effective translation strategies and creative solutions to translation issues.
Employ key strategies to translate specialised and creative texts, such as audiovisual and advertisement materials.

assessment_summary.title: Presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: 2/04/2026 - 28/05/2026; Presentation 1: Week 6 (Group 1) and 7 (Group 2); Presentation 2: Week 12 (Group 2) and 13 (Group 1)

assessment_details.title: Participation
assessment_details.task_description: Students are expected to actively participate in class discussions using the translations they have prepared and brought to class (submission not required). In Weeks 2–5 and 8–11, tutorials will include group discussions of the assigned translations. Students will need to prepare for the tutorials by (1) revisiting the lecture content and (2) preparing draft translations of the assigned source texts. Bringing your own notes and translations is highly encouraged. Contributions to the discussions will be marked as follows (1 mark per week): 1 mark – Student shows clear engagement with and understanding of the weekly content 0.5 marks – Student shows some engagement with and understanding of the weekly content 0 marks – Student does not engage and, thus, shows no to very limited engagement with and understanding of the weekly content The total of 8 marks will count as the maximum final percentage 5% of the course assessment. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This assessment task is designed to facilitate students’ development of creative translation skills and is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted during class discussion. Any attempted use of AI or MT in class may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Students may, however, find it useful to use those tools to plan and prepare for their class discussion, while noting that this task is designed to be challenging, authentic, and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This assessment will be done in-person with no submission necessary.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This is a participation assessment to facilitate students’ development of creative translation skills through in-depth discussion of the translation process, from source-text analysis to creative translation solutions. The assessment task is designed to assess students’ preparation, engagement and active participation in class discussion, which is difficult to recreate outside the classroom. Each class includes specific activities that allow the teaching team to track students’ participation and engagement to fulfil the marking criteria outlined above. These activities are made available to students regardless of their presence in class (via Blackboard course site). Students have ongoing access to their participation marks throughout the semester. Furthermore, there will be 8 opportunities for weekly in-class discussion (1 mark per week).
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 2/04/2026 - 28/05/2026 Presentation 1: Week 6 (Group 1) and 7 (Group 2) Presentation 2: Week 12 (Group 2) and 13 (Group 1)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is an individual presentation assessment. Students will be divided into two groups, Group 1 and Group 2, and assigned to the week when they present their work. Presentation 1 - 25% Choose a source text in English to translate into Chinese/Japanese. The source material must be audiovisual, promotional/advertisement OR literary and must not have been translated into your Chinese/Japanese before, i.e., its translation must not be publicly available. The length of the source text may vary. You do not need to translate a whole book, for example, but you should translate a sufficient volume for this assessment task. While translating the source text, be conscious of your decision-making process in handling various translation-related issues, such as the context of the source text and its translation, culturally specific and/or linguistically challenging expressions, and stylistic issues. Present an analysis of your translation and your translation process/strategies in English. The analysis should explain how you handled the translation issues and developed creative and effective solutions to the issues, with reference to constraints and/or requirements relevant to the source text. Each presentation should be no more than 8 minutes, followed by a 5-minute Q&A session. Students are expected to demonstrate their critical engagement with the task by effectively presenting their work and providing insightful answers to the questions asked in the Q&A session. Use PowerPoint slides to present your work and submit the slides after the presentation. Presentation 2 - 25% Choose a source text in Chinese/Japanese to translate into English. The source material must be audiovisual, promotional/advertisement OR literary and must not have been translated into English before, i.e., its translation must not be publicly available. Choose material in a different field from the one you used in Presentation 1. If you worked on a literary text in Presentation 1, for example, you must translate either audiovisual or promotional/advertisement material in Presentation 2. The length of the source text may vary. You do not need to translate a whole book, for example, but you should translate a sufficient volume for this assessment task. While translating the source text, be conscious of your decision-making process in handling various translation-related issues, such as the context of the source text and its translation, culturally specific and/or linguistically challenging expressions, and stylistic issues. Present an analysis of your translation and translation process/strategies in English. The analysis should explain how you handled the translation issues and developed creative and effective solutions to the issues, with reference to constraints and/or requirements relevant to the source text. Each presentation should be no more than 8 minutes, followed by a 5-minute Q&A session. Students are expected to demonstrate their critical engagement with the task by effectively presenting their work and providing insightful answers to the questions asked in the Q&A session. Use PowerPoint slides to present your work and submit the slides after the presentation. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted in the presentation venue. Any attempted use of AI or MT during the presentation may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Students may, however, find it useful to use those tools to plan and prepare for their presentation, while noting that this task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. Failure to reference the use of generative AI or MT at the end of the PowerPoint presentation slides may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their presentation independent of AI and MT tools. Remember that this assessment item is about presenting your work, NOT about performing a script. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your presentation slides via Turnitin.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Reflective essay on creative translation
assessment_details.task_description: Write a reflective essay on the translation of course materials between Weeks 1 and 13, excluding your presentation materials. The essay should explain how you handled various translation issues you encountered and how you developed creative and effective solutions to the issues, with reference to constraints and/or requirements relevant to the source text. It should also contain discussions of specific comprehension problems and specific target-language problems and include your observations about how you improved in these areas over the semester. You do not need to refer to all course materials in your essay, but your discussion of specific translation examples must cover the three areas of audiovisual translation, translation of advertisement/promotional material, and literary translation. Length: 2000 words plus or minus a 10% allowance, excluding any captions, diagrams, tables, reference list or appendices. State the number of words at the end of your essay. The essay should: be typed on A4 paper (1.5 line spacing); use 12-point Calibri font; include any tables or diagrams in their appropriate position in the text, not at the end; demonstrate familiarity with relevant literature and an ability to evaluate the material critically; use the APA referencing style; and acknowledge when other people’s ideas are used. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
